meaning: adj.严厉的,迫切的,银根紧的
examples: He announced that there would be more stringent controls on the possession of weapons.
Stringent job dismissal regulations adversely affect productivity growth and hamper both prosperity and overall well-being.
"In some cases the quality requirements are so stringent that even if you wanted to have a human do the job, you couldn't," Burnstein says.
Thanks to stringent federal standards, average fuel economy reached an all-time high in 2012.
Management needs to know that IT has implemented and is enforcing stringent IAM systems and protocols.
